title: Smart Interface Design Patterns — Vitaly Friedman (Smashing Magazine)
insights: Vitaly Friedman's 40-lesson course covers 100 patterns with live examples and 15h video. Pattern categories: navigation, forms, tables, data display, feedback, e-commerce, search, accessibility. Course includes 165-page UX checklist deck updated annually. Key patterns: complex data tables with nested filters, combobox vs multi-select vs listbox decision tree, autocomplete UX (5-step framework), mega-menus, infinite scroll vs pagination.

title: Autocomplete UX: Five Simple Steps — Smart Interface Design Patterns
insights: 5-step autocomplete framework: (1) Show max 8 suggestions (not 10+), (2) Highlight query in suggestion, (3) Show category/type label with suggestion, (4) Include recent searches at top, (5) Keyboard nav (arrow keys + Enter). Enterprise autocomplete: show entity type icon, include people/docs/tasks cross-entity search. Do not show typos in recent search history — show corrected query.

title: Combobox vs. Multiselect vs. Listbox — Smart Interface Design Patterns
insights: Decision tree: listbox (≤7 options, always visible), dropdown select (7-15 options, space constrained), combobox (15+ options, type-to-filter), multiselect combobox (multiple values from large set). Multiselect pattern: selected values shown as tags/chips above or inside input. Combobox: WAI-ARIA combobox role, aria-autocomplete, aria-expanded. Never use native <select multiple> in enterprise apps.

title: Data Table Design UX Patterns — Pencil & Paper
insights: Pencil & Paper enterprise data table analysis: sorting (column chevrons, secondary sort via Shift+click), bulk selection (header checkbox = page, toolbar action = apply), inline editing (cell-level vs row-level), row expansion (expandable rows for related data), column resizing, column reordering, sticky first/last columns, empty state, and loading skeleton. Anti-pattern: putting bulk actions inside row dropdown menus.

title: Filter UX Design Patterns — Pencil & Paper
insights: Pencil & Paper filter pattern analysis: side panel (best for 5+ filter categories), top horizontal bar (3-5 quick filters), inline column filters (table-specific). Applied filter chips below filter bar. Search within filter panel for long lists. Batch vs instant apply: batch for performance-heavy results, instant for <200ms response. Hierarchical filters (parent/child category). Mobile: filter drawer (full-screen slide-up).

title: Dashboard Design UX Patterns — Pencil & Paper
insights: Pencil & Paper dashboard patterns: overview cards (KPI strip at top, 4-6 max), trend charts (sparkline or mini line chart in card), alert/notification section (at-a-glance status), data table (secondary detail). Widget grid: draggable/resizable for customizable dashboards. F-pattern layout: most critical metric top-left. Anti-pattern: 'data vomit' — too many metrics without hierarchy.

title: Mobile Filter UX Patterns — Pencil & Paper
insights: Mobile filter patterns: full-screen filter drawer (swipe-up/button trigger), chips row (horizontal scroll, visible applied filters), sort+filter combined bottom sheet. Mobile-specific: show applied filter count on trigger button, 'Show results (N)' confirmation button. No instant-apply on mobile — batch apply on close. Sticky filter bar on scroll for always-accessible refinement.

title: Empty State UX Examples and Design Rules — Eleken
insights: Eleken analysis of 30+ real SaaS empty states. Key finding: best empty states use action-oriented headlines ('Start by adding your first candidate') not declarative ones ('No candidates yet'). Illustration style: abstract/geometric beats photorealistic for empty states. First-time empty states: celebratory tone. Filter/search no-results: problem-solving tone ('Try removing some filters'). Never show empty state for load errors — use error state instead.

title: Wizard UI Pattern — Eleken
insights: Wizard pattern analysis with 20+ real examples. When to use: multi-variable setup (3+ interdependent fields), legal/compliance forms, onboarding (personalization required). When NOT to use: simple 1-2 field forms, repeated daily tasks, when users need to review all fields together. Personalisation wizards: branch on user answers to skip irrelevant steps (conditional logic).

title: Bulk Action UX: 8 Design Guidelines for SaaS — Eleken
insights: 8 SaaS bulk action guidelines: (1) Floating bottom toolbar pattern beats static toolbar for large tables. (2) Show '{N} selected' counter. (3) Allow select all across pages. (4) Group actions by type (primary, secondary, destructive). (5) Disable unavailable bulk actions contextually. (6) Undo toast after destructive bulk action. (7) Progress indicator for long-running bulk ops. (8) Empty selection: toolbar disappears.

title: User Onboarding UX Patterns — Eleken
insights: SaaS onboarding patterns: product tour (step-by-step UI walkthrough via tooltips), interactive checklist (persistent sidebar widget, tasks with checkmarks + completion %), empty state onboarding (first-use empty state with inline setup prompt), modal-based welcome flow (3-5 step full-screen wizard on first login). Best performers: checklist (72% completion rate) > product tour (38%). Always allow skip.

title: User Onboarding UI/UX Patterns — Appcues
insights: Appcues onboarding pattern taxonomy: Modals (welcome, feature announcement), Tooltips (contextual hotspot, progressive), Slideouts (side panel tutorial), Checklists (sticky widget with tasks), Driven actions (highlight + restrict navigation), Empty states with CTAs (setup prompts in context). Key principle: get users to 'aha moment' within first session. Personalization via signup survey reduces time-to-value by 48%.

title: Autocomplete Design: 9 UX Best Practices — Baymard Institute
insights: Baymard research (18,000+ user tests): only 19% of sites implement all best practices. Critical findings: (1) Show query match highlighted in suggestion, (2) Show category/type label ('in Electronics'), (3) Differentiate search term suggestions from product name suggestions, (4) Show image thumbnails for product autocomplete, (5) Max 8 suggestions, (6) Don't autocomplete typos into final query. Qualitative finding: users trust highlighted matches more.

title: Checkout UX Best Practices 2025 — Baymard Institute
insights: Baymard 2025 checkout research: average checkout has 11.3 fields (optimal is ~8). Multi-step checkout (address → shipping → payment → review) outperforms accordion checkout for new users. Progress indicators reduce abandonment. Field formatting: card number should auto-space every 4 digits, expiry as MM/YY not MM and YYYY. 35% conversion improvement possible from checkout UX fixes alone.

title: Paywall UX Design Best Practices — Webuild
insights: Paywall pattern anatomy: feature preview (show what's locked), value proposition (3-5 bullet points of premium benefits), pricing table (2-3 tiers max, highlight recommended), CTA button (trial or subscribe), trust signals (logos, testimonials, money-back). Soft paywall converts 3.2x better than hard. Contextual paywall (triggered at point of blocked action) converts 2.1x better than dedicated upgrade page.

title: Onboarding UX Patterns — Chameleon
insights: Chameleon onboarding pattern detail: Checklist widget = persistent element (bottom-left corner), shows tasks with completion state. Tooltip hotspot = pulsing dot on UI element, click to reveal guidance. Modal flow = full-screen overlay, 3-5 steps, progress dots. Slideout = 300px side panel, triggered by action. Tooltip vs modal: tooltips for contextual help, modals for new feature announcements. Conditional branching based on user role.

title: Empty State UI Pattern — Mobbin
insights: Mobbin empty state gallery: 400+ real-world empty state screens from 1150+ apps. Pattern findings: consumer apps favor illustrated empty states with character/mascot; enterprise apps favor icon + text + CTA. Dark mode empty states use lighter illustrations. Mobile empty states: illustration fills 40-50% of screen, centered. Web empty states: centered within content area, never full-viewport. Animated empty states (Lottie) used by 23% of top apps.

title: Command Palette UI Design — Mobbin
insights: Mobbin command palette analysis from 200+ real apps. Activation: Cmd+K (59% of apps), Cmd+P (Visual Studio Code influence), Cmd+/ (contextual). Anatomy: search input (autofocus), grouped results (sections: Recent, Commands, Files, People), keyboard navigation (arrow keys + Enter). Enterprise apps: show keyboard shortcut for each command. Max results visible: 7-8 before scroll. Placeholder text = 'Search actions, files, people...'

title: Date Picker UI Design — Mobbin
insights: Mobbin date picker analysis: calendar grid (most common, 78%), wheel/scroll (iOS native, 22%), free text input (always as fallback). Date range: dual calendar (side-by-side months) on desktop, single calendar with range highlighting on mobile. Presets strip above calendar: 'Today', 'This week', 'This month', 'Custom'. Disabled dates: grayed out, not removed. Clear button in top-right corner.

title: Card UI Design — Mobbin
insights: Mobbin card analysis: card anatomy = media (top), title + metadata (middle), actions (bottom). Card click = navigate to detail. 3 content density variants: compact (title only), standard (title + description + 1 action), expanded (full metadata + 2 actions). Cards used for: content browsing (news, products), resource grids (files, projects), people directories (team, candidates). Hover state: shadow lift + border highlight.

title: Skeleton UI Design — Mobbin
insights: Mobbin skeleton analysis: 85% of top apps use skeleton screens vs spinners. Shimmer direction: left-to-right (63%), top-to-bottom (22%), radial (15%). Color: gray-200 base, gray-100 shimmer (light mode); gray-700 base, gray-600 shimmer (dark mode). Line placeholder widths: vary intentionally (50-90%) to look natural. Avatar placeholder: circular. Never use skeleton for sub-200ms loads (visual noise).

title: Accordion UI Design — Mobbin
insights: Mobbin accordion patterns: chevron indicator (right-aligned, 78%); plus/minus indicator (15%); arrow (7%). Expand/collapse animation: max-height transition (smooth) vs display none toggle (instant). Mobile accordions: full-width, chevron right. Desktop accordions: may be partial-width. Single vs multi-expand: settings pages usually single-open, FAQ pages usually multi-open. Divider between items: 1px border.

title: Navigation Pattern — ServiceNow Horizon
insights: Horizon (ServiceNow) navigation pattern: Workspace app = left nav bar (collapsed 56px icon-only, expanded 240px icon+label) + top navigation bar (app switcher, user menu, global search). Patterns = 'recipes' distinct from components. Navigation levels: L1 (sidebar icons), L2 (expanded section), L3 (contextual side panel). AI integration: AI-powered quick actions in navigation panel.

title: Kanban (Visual Board) Pattern — ServiceNow Horizon
insights: Horizon Visual Board (Kanban): columns = workflow stages. Card = entity (incident, request, candidate). Card surface: title, assignee avatar, priority icon, due date, tag chips (max 3). Drag and drop: cards reorder within column and across columns. Column WIP limit: visual indicator when limit exceeded (column header turns orange). Quick actions on card: reassign, escalate, close. Swimlanes optional for grouping by team.

title: UI Component Examples Catalog — Nicelydone
insights: Nicelydone: 14,300+ real SaaS UI components from 214,200+ screens. Pattern categories with example counts (2025): AI components (277+), Modal (116+), Tabs (283+), Bento grid (259+), Timeline/History (155+), Autocomplete (4+), Settings pages (4167+). Weekly updated. Used by design teams at enterprise SaaS. Best source for 'what do real apps do' validation of pattern choices.

title: Timeline & History UI Examples — Nicelydone
insights: Nicelydone timeline gallery (155+ examples): vertical timeline most common. Anatomy: connector line (left-aligned vertical), event node (icon or dot), timestamp (right-aligned), event description. Grouping: by day (most common), by actor, by type. Expandable events: show truncated description, expand to full detail. Activity feed variant: real-time updates, newest first. Audit log variant: immutable, includes 'who changed what from X to Y'.

title: Timeline Pattern — UX Patterns for Developers
insights: UX Patterns dev timeline pattern: three types: vertical summary (compact list, date + icon + description), horizontal (milestones along time axis), swimlane (multiple actors, Gantt-like). Implementation considerations: lazy-load for long timelines, grouping reduces cognitive load, filter/search on large timelines, 'show more' expansion pattern. Real-time feed: WebSocket update inserts new item at top with subtle animation.

title: Atlassian Design System Overview — Design Systems Surf
insights: Atlassian DS overview: 50+ components, pattern library for Jira/Confluence/Trello. 2025 typography refresh (bolder fonts). Key patterns: issue creation flow (quick-create modal vs full-page), page tree navigation (Confluence), board view (Jira Kanban), backlog prioritization. AI patterns: Rovo chat interface, AI suggestion inline in editor. Atlassian uses progressive enhancement: basic functionality without JS, enhanced with JS.

title: Essential Resources for Complex Data Tables — Stéphanie Walter
insights: Curated resource collection for enterprise data tables. Key referenced patterns: fixed vs flexible column widths, right-align numbers/currency, left-align text, center-align icons/status. Responsive table strategies: horizontal scroll (simplest), priority columns (hide low-priority on mobile), card transformation (rows become cards). Sticky header + first column for large grids. Row height: compact (32px), default (48px), relaxed (64px).

title: Refactoring UI — Adam Wathan & Steve Schoger
insights: Refactoring UI core principles: design in grayscale first (forces hierarchy via spacing/contrast/typography before color), build 5-9 shade palettes per hue, constrained spacing scale (4/8/12/16/24/32/48/64/96/128px), type scale (12/14/16/18/20/24/30/36/48/60/72px). Pattern library includes 20+ component categories, 200+ component styles. 'Start with too much whitespace' — remove, never add.

title: Data Table — shadcn/ui
insights: shadcn/ui data table: headless composition via TanStack Table + shadcn Table component. Standard composition: column definitions (with cell renderers, header renderers, sort config), toolbar (Input search + DropdownMenu column visibility), data rows, footer pagination. Faceted filter popover pattern: DropdownMenuCheckboxItem per filter option, Badge showing selection count. Enterprise variant: server-side sort/filter/paginate, React.useReducer for state.

title: Sidebar Component — shadcn/ui
insights: shadcn Sidebar: built as a collapsible composition using Collapsible + Sheet (mobile). Sidebar states: expanded (240px, icon+label), collapsed (64px, icon only), mobile (Sheet overlay). Keyboard shortcut: Cmd+B to toggle. SidebarProvider wraps app for context. SidebarMenu, SidebarMenuItem, SidebarMenuButton sub-components. Mobile: sidebar becomes Sheet drawer. Preserves collapse state via cookie.

title: Design Patterns Overview — Ant Design
insights: Ant Design pattern documentation: 6 page templates (home, dashboard, list, table, details, form). Pattern categories: navigation design (standard layouts), data entry (form types), data display (table, card, list), feedback (modal, notification, message), and overview pages. Ant Design Pro (out-of-box): 20+ full page templates including dashboard, list, form, detail, exception pages. Enterprise-focused: built for internal tools and admin panels.

title: Detail Page Pattern — Ant Design
insights: Ant Design detail page pattern: PageHeader (breadcrumb + title + subtitle + tags + actions) + content area. Content layout: basic info section (descriptive list or form), table section (related records), tab section (multiple categories of related info). Action placement: primary actions in PageHeader, secondary actions in tabs. Edit mode: inline form replaces description list. Mobile: PageHeader stacks vertically.

title: Layout — Ant Design
insights: Ant Design 6 layout templates: (1) Top navigation + content area, (2) Left sidebar + content, (3) Left sidebar + top nav + content, (4) Top nav + left sidebar + content, (5) Inline menu (no sidebar, flat), (6) Top nav + mixed (complex). Grid: 24-column, gutter 8/16/24px. Breakpoints: xs<576, sm≥576, md≥768, lg≥992, xl≥1200, xxl≥1600.

title: Ant Design Pro — GitHub
insights: Ant Design Pro: 20+ page templates, 10+ business components. Templates: dashboard (analytics + monitor + workplace), form (basic, step-by-step, advanced), list (standard, card, search), profile (basic, advanced), result (success, fail), exception (403, 404, 500), account (center, settings). 17k+ GitHub stars. Business components: comments, footer toolbar, exception, tag select, standard table.

title: Awesome Design Systems — alexpate (GitHub)
insights: Curated list of 200+ design systems with 21.8k GitHub stars. Categorized by: open source vs proprietary, web vs mobile, industry. Notable pattern-rich systems cataloged: Polaris (Shopify), Carbon (IBM), Atlassian, Primer (GitHub), Lightning (Salesforce), Spectrum (Adobe), Fluent (Microsoft), PatternFly (Red Hat), Helios (HashiCorp), Cloudscape (AWS), USWDS (US Gov), GOV.UK, Horizon (ServiceNow).

title: Carbon Design System — IBM (GitHub)
insights: Carbon GitHub repo: 7.9k stars. v11 active, v10 in maintenance. React components + Vanilla + Angular + Vue wrappers. Pattern documentation in Storybook and carbondesignsystem.com. Pattern contribution guide: new patterns require a problem statement, usage examples, accessibility audit, and content guidance before merging. Community patterns maintained separately at carbondesignsystem.com/community/patterns/.

title: Helios Design System — HashiCorp (GitHub)
insights: Helios GitHub repo (hashicorp/design-system). Ember.js + React components. Patterns documented: table multi-select (3 selection scopes), form patterns (8px grid spacing, label-above convention), navigation (sidebar + top bar). Components: HDS prefix. Figma: HDS Product Foundations v2.0 + HDS Product Patterns v2.0 (detachable templates). Used across HashiCorp Cloud Platform products.

title: Figma Community Libraries — Free Template Libraries
insights: Figma Community: 4,700+ UI kits, 1,000+ template libraries. Top pattern-focused libraries: Untitled UI (320k+ users, 10k components), TapTap DS (dark mode, Auto Layout 5.0), shadcn/ui kit (code export plugin). Pattern recommendation by use: SaaS = Untitled UI; Enterprise = Ant Design or Carbon kit; Government = USWDS kit. Figma now supports component properties, variables for theming, and advanced prototyping for pattern documentation.

title: 150+ Free Stepper/Wizard Component Types — Figma Community
insights: Figma resource: 150+ stepper/wizard component variants. Categories: horizontal stepper (numbered, labeled, icon), vertical stepper (left-nav pattern), breadcrumb stepper, progress bar variant, dot indicator. States: inactive, active, completed, error. Mobile variants included. Demonstrates the breadth of wizard pattern implementations across industries. Used as reference for custom pattern implementations.

title: Dialog (Modal) Pattern — WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide
insights: W3C WAI-ARIA APG modal pattern: role=dialog, aria-modal=true, aria-labelledby (header), aria-describedby (body). Focus management: focus moves to first focusable element on open, Tab cycles within dialog, Escape closes. Background content: aria-hidden=true. Focus returns to trigger element on close. Two variants: dialog (modal) and alertdialog (for confirmations requiring immediate response). Code examples with focus trap implementation.

title: Combobox Pattern — WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide
insights: W3C WAI-ARIA combobox pattern: role=combobox on input, aria-expanded, aria-autocomplete (none/list/inline/both), aria-controls (listbox ID). Keyboard: Down arrow opens/moves to first option, Enter selects, Escape closes. Autocomplete variants: list (shows options), inline (completes in input field), both. Required for all custom select/autocomplete components. Pattern includes complete code example.

title: Design Patterns — Smashing Magazine
insights: Smashing Magazine design patterns category: high-quality research-backed articles. Key patterns covered (2024-2025): mega-menus, complex data tables, mobile filter patterns, notification UX timing, date/time pickers, command palette evolution, AI chat interface patterns, onboarding flows. Monthly pattern deep-dives. Smart Interface Design Patterns course (Vitaly Friedman) paired with article content.

title: Designing Effective Breadcrumbs — Smashing Magazine
insights: Smashing Magazine breadcrumb deep-dive: 3 types (location, path, attribute). Location breadcrumbs are universally applicable. Attribute breadcrumbs (show product filters) degrade to location breadcrumbs on mobile. Separator choice: '>' most discoverable for users. Schema.org breadcrumb markup for SEO. Never truncate breadcrumbs on mobile — use back button pattern instead. ARIA: nav role + aria-label='Breadcrumb' + aria-current='page' on last item.

title: Date Picker Design — UX Collective
insights: UX Collective date picker analysis: grid calendar beats wheel for date range selection (easier to grasp start/end). Mandatory fields: always provide free-text input alongside calendar. Keyboard: arrow keys navigate calendar cells, Enter selects, Page Up/Down changes month. Date range: first click = start, second click = end, highlighted in between. Time picker: separate from date picker (avoid combined complex component).

title: How to Build a Remarkable Command Palette — Superhuman
insights: Superhuman (the email app famous for its UX) on command palettes: fuzzy matching should rank by (1) exact prefix match, (2) word-start match, (3) arbitrary position match. Show keyboard shortcut for each command so users discover shortcuts. Group commands: navigation, actions, settings. Recent commands prioritized. Empty state: show most-used commands. Cmd+K as the universal trigger in modern apps.

title: Command Palette Interfaces — Philip Davis
insights: Analysis of command palette evolution: originated in Sublime Text (Cmd+Shift+P), adopted by VS Code, Linear, Notion, Figma, Vercel dashboard. Pattern anatomy: modal overlay (full or centered floating), search input (autofocus, placeholder 'Search commands...'), result groups (Recent, Commands, Navigation), keyboard shortcut hints. Anti-pattern: hiding palette activation trigger — must be discoverable via UI button for new users.

title: Kanban Board in Recruitment — Treegarden
insights: HR-domain Kanban pattern: recruitment pipeline stages = Applied, Screening, Interview 1, Interview 2, Offer, Hired/Rejected. Candidate card surface: name, role, days-in-stage (highlighted if >SLA), status badge, pending actions icon. WIP limits per stage reduce bottlenecks. Quick actions on card: move to next stage, schedule interview, reject, add note. Bulk move via drag-select + column drop. Swimlanes by hiring manager or job req.

title: Command Palette UX Patterns — Alicja Suska
insights: Command palette UX taxonomy: (1) Global commands (open document, switch workspace), (2) Contextual commands (actions on selected item), (3) Navigation shortcuts (go to page), (4) Settings shortcuts. Result row anatomy: icon + label + keyboard shortcut + category tag. Empty state: 'No results for X — try Y' with suggestions. Accessibility: role=listbox, each result role=option, aria-activedescendant.

title: Designing Better Destructive Action Modals — UX Psychology
insights: Destructive modal patterns: standard 2-button (Cancel + Delete), confirmation input (type resource name to confirm, e.g., GitHub repo delete), checkbox acknowledgment ('I understand this cannot be undone'), delayed enable (confirm button enabled after 3s countdown). Research: confirmation input reduces accidental deletes by 87%. Use confirmation input for irreversible high-stakes actions only (not for recoverable deletions).
